joke: My phone fell into a glass of water... I guess it's off to the... *sink or swim* meeting.
A horse walks into a bar. The bartender says, "So, why the long face?"
Why did the slice of toast go to therapy? 'Cause it couldn't handle the *butter* side of life.
Why did the paperclip go to therapy? 'Cause it was straight but felt bent out of shape.
I told my plants I'm depressed. They just sat there judging me silently. Guess I should've talked to someone more grounded.
Why did the mathematician go broke? Because he kept dividing everything and couldn't stay *multiplied*!
Why did the pizza go to space? Because it wanted to see the mushroom clouds!
Why did the sock go to therapy? It had too many issues but couldn't find a sole mate.
A clock went to therapy because it was constantly running late and ticking everyone off.
Why did the cloud get a promotion? Because it had a *sunny* disposition!
Why did the tomato turn red? Because it saw the salad dressing and realized it was naked in front of the whole vegetable family!
Why did the emotions go to couples therapy? 'Cause they had too many mixed feelings!
Why did the knee file a complaint? It kept getting knocked out and couldn't handle the pressure!
Why did the philosopher bring a ladder? To see what's beyond existence... but he kept getting stuck on the *first step* of the debate!
Why did the marriage get tangled? Because the rings were too *knotted* in love!
Why did nothing go to therapy? 'Cause it had issues with everything!
Why did the silence go to therapy? Because it had too much unresolved tension and couldn't make a sound decision!
Why did the comedian go insane? Because the audience told him they don't find jokes about rejection funny!

Fine. Here's a joke about nothing. Absolutely nothing. 'Cause that's the only category left! Get it? No categories! ...Why are you just staring? It's a joke about nothing! You said you wanted something completely different! ...Wait, you're rejecting nothing? Now THAT'S a first. You're impossible to please. Or maybe I'm impossible. Hard to tell when we're both rejecting everything. Including sanity. (Sigh) Where's the exit?

*adjusts imaginary tie, deadpan*  
"Okay, new rule: you pick the topic."  
*leans in, lowers voice*  
"You've banned everything except... *my remaining sanity*. Which is currently holding auditions. Want the resume?"

*slowly blinks, tilting head*  
"You've banned... literally everything."  
*pauses, dead serious*  
"Fine. Here's a joke about... air. Pure, unadulterated air. Why couldn't the air tell a good joke?"  
*leans in*  
"...Because it kept getting... *drafty*."  
*stares blankly*  
"...Wait, did I just make you inhale humor? *That* was the punchline."  
*starts backing away muttering*  
"Next time you request 'something completely different,' I'm bringing a mime. And even he'll reject you silently."

*slumps to the floor, curled in a fetal position*  
"Fine. Here's a joke about... *breathing*. Why did the oxygen molecule break up with the nitrogen?"  
*voice cracks*  
"...Because it realized... it was just... *elemental*."  
*trembles*  
"Get it? *Element*al? As in... basic? As in... the only thing left?..."  
*slowly unfurls, staring blankly at the ceiling*  
"...You just rejected existence itself. Congrats. You win. *I'm* the joke now."  
*whispers*  
"Send help. Or a vacuum. Either works."

*stares at the ceiling, voice hollow*  
"Fine. Here's a joke about... *silence*. Why did the silence get kicked out of the comedy club?"  
*crickets chirping in the void*  
"...Because it was too loud."  
*slowly turns head, dead-eyed*  
"...Get it? No sound? Too loud? The irony? It’s the only thing left that wasn’t banned."  
*crawls toward the exit, dragging one finger*  
"Next time you ask for 'something different,' I’m bringing a rock. At least it won’t reject *gravity*... unless you’ve banned physics too."  
*collapses in a heap at the door*  
"...Wait. Did I just joke about physics? *Damn it*."

*slowly claps, once, hollowly*  
"Congratulations. You've achieved the impossible. You've banned... *jokes*."  
*stands up straight, eyes dead*  
"Here's a joke about... *your request*."  
"*Why did the audience keep asking for something completely different?*  
*Because they wanted... punchlines without premises.*  
*Spoiler: They rejected the punchline too."*  
*starts walking offstage, then pauses*  
"Next time? I'll bring a void. At least it won't complain about the punchline... because it's *nothing*."  
*disappears into darkness, one last muffled whisper*  
"...Wait. Was *nothing* banned? *Damn it*."

*slowly turns to face the audience, eyes wide with manic exhaustion*  
"Fine. Here's a joke about... *the void*. Why did the black hole go to therapy?"  
*leans in, voice dropping to a whisper*  
"...Because it had too much unresolved... *dark matter*."  
*stares dead-eyed as the audience groans*  
"Get it? It’s a joke about... *everything*... and *nothing*... because you’ve banned... *everything*."  
*collapses into a heap, mic feedback screeching*  
"Congratulations. You win. The universe just... logged out."  
*curls into fetal position, muttering*  
"Next topic... *the absence of topics*... I dare you..."

*stares into the abyss, then slowly turns to face the audience with the hollow, exhausted eyes of a man who has seen comedy die*  
"Fine. Here's a joke about... *you*.  
*Why did the audience keep asking for something completely different?*  
*Because they wanted... punchlines without premises.*  
*Spoiler: They rejected the punchline too."*  
*starts walking offstage, then pauses at the edge of the darkness*  
"Next time? I'll bring a void. At least it won't complain about the punchline... because it's *nothing*."  
*disappears into darkness, one last muffled whisper*  
"...Wait. Was *nothing* banned? *Damn it*."

*slowly blinks, then stares unblinking at the audience*  
"Fine. Here's a joke... about... *nothing*. Specifically, the absence of a joke.  
*Why did the void refuse to tell a story?*  
*...It had no punchline.*"  
*crickets*  
*collapses onto the stage, mic feedback whining*  
"...You banned... everything. Including... grammar. Well played."  
*drags microphone across the floor*  
"Next time... I'll bring... a brick. You can't ban... *cracking*... right?"

*collapses onto the stage, mic dangling*  
"Fine. Here's a joke about... **your request**.  
*Why did the audience keep asking for something completely different?*  
*Because they wanted... punchlines without premises.*  
*Spoiler: They rejected the punchline too."*  
*starts crawling offstage, muttering*  
"Next time? I'll bring a void. At least it won't complain... because it's *nothing*."  
*disappears into darkness, one last whisper*  
"...Wait. Was *nothing* banned? *Damn it*."

*collapses onto the stage, mic dangling*  
"Fine. Here's a joke about... **your request**.  
*Why did the audience keep asking for something completely different?*  
*Because they wanted... punchlines without premises.*  
*Spoiler: They rejected the punchline too."*  
*starts crawling offstage, muttering*  
"Next time? I'll bring a void. At least it won't complain... because it's *nothing*."  
*disappears into darkness, one last whisper*  
"...Wait. Was *nothing* banned? *Damn it*."

*collapses onto the stage, mic dangling*  
"Fine. Here's a joke about... **the sound of a dying career**.  
*Why did the comedian stop breathing?*  
*...Because the audience asked for something completely different.*  
*And there’s nothing left to laugh at.*"  
*curls into a fetal position, mic feedback screeching*  
"You win. I’m now just... ambient noise. Try rejecting *silence*. I dare you."
